title: EgoActor: Grounding Task Planning into Spatial-aware Egocentric Actions for Humanoid Robots via Visual-Language Models
summary: Deploying humanoid robots in real-world settings is fundamentally challenging, as it demands tight integration of perception, locomotion, and manipulation under partial-information observations and dynamically changing environments. As well as transitioning robustly between sub-tasks of different types. Towards addressing these challenges, we propose a novel task - EgoActing, which requires directly grounding high-level instructions into various, precise, spatially aware humanoid actions. We further instantiate this task by introducing EgoActor, a unified and scalable vision-language model (VLM) that can predict locomotion primitives (e.g., walk, turn, move sideways, change height), head movements, manipulation commands, and human-robot interactions to coordinate perception and execution in real-time. We leverage broad supervision over egocentric RGB-only data from real-world demonstrations, spatial reasoning question-answering, and simulated environment demonstrations, enabling EgoActor to make robust, context-aware decisions and perform fluent action inference (under 1s) with both 8B and 4B parameter models. Extensive evaluations in both simulated and real-world environments demonstrate that EgoActor effectively bridges abstract task planning and concrete motor execution, while generalizing across diverse tasks and unseen environments.
tldr: The paper introduces EgoActor, a vision-language model that grounds high-level instructions into spatial-aware egocentric actions for humanoid robots, enabling them to perform diverse tasks in real-world environments by integrating perception, locomotion, and manipulation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了EgoActor，一个视觉语言模型，可以将高层次指令转化为类人机器人的空间感知自我中心动作，从而使他们能够在现实世界环境中执行各种任务，整合感知、运动和操作。
summary_zh: 在真实世界环境中部署人形机器人极具挑战性，因为它需要在部分信息观测和动态变化的环境下，紧密集成感知、运动和操作能力，同时需要能够稳健地在不同类型的子任务之间切换。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一项新任务——EgoActing，该任务要求将高级指令直接转化为各种精确的、具有空间感知能力的人形机器人动作。此外，我们通过引入EgoActor来实现这项任务，EgoActor是一种统一且可扩展的视觉-语言模型（VLM），它可以预测运动基元（例如，行走、转弯、侧移、改变高度）、头部运动、操作指令和人机交互，从而实时协调感知和执行过程。我们利用来自真实演示的以自我为中心的RGB单一数据、空间推理问答和模拟环境演示的广泛监督，使EgoActor能够做出稳健的、上下文感知的决策，并使用80亿和40亿参数模型进行流畅的动作推断（小于1秒）。在模拟和真实世界环境中的大量评估表明，EgoActor有效地桥接了抽象任务规划和具体运动执行，同时能够泛化到各种任务和未见环境中。

title: GeneralVLA: Generalizable Vision-Language-Action Models with Knowledge-Guided Trajectory Planning
summary: Large foundation models have shown strong open-world generalization to complex problems in vision and language, but similar levels of generalization have yet to be achieved in robotics. One fundamental challenge is that the models exhibit limited zero-shot capability, which hampers their ability to generalize effectively to unseen scenarios. In this work, we propose GeneralVLA (Generalizable Vision-Language-Action Models with Knowledge-Guided Trajectory Planning), a hierarchical vision-language-action (VLA) model that can be more effective in utilizing the generalization of foundation models, enabling zero-shot manipulation and automatically generating data for robotics. In particular, we study a class of hierarchical VLA model where the high-level ASM (Affordance Segmentation Module) is finetuned to perceive image keypoint affordances of the scene; the mid-level 3DAgent carries out task understanding, skill knowledge, and trajectory planning to produce a 3D path indicating the desired robot end-effector trajectory. The intermediate 3D path prediction is then served as guidance to the low-level, 3D-aware control policy capable of precise manipulation. Compared to alternative approaches, our method requires no real-world robotic data collection or human demonstration, making it much more scalable to diverse tasks and viewpoints. Empirically, GeneralVLA successfully generates trajectories for 14 tasks, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods such as VoxPoser. The generated demonstrations can train more robust behavior cloning policies than training with human demonstrations or from data generated by VoxPoser, Scaling-up, and Code-As-Policies. We believe GeneralVLA can be the scalable method for both generating data for robotics and solving novel tasks in a zero-shot setting. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/GeneralVLA. Website: https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/GeneralVLA.
tldr: GeneralVLA introduces a hierarchical vision-language-action model that leverages foundation model generalization and knowledge-guided trajectory planning for robotic manipulation, demonstrating strong zero-shot performance and autonomous data generation capabilities without real-world data collection.
tldr_zh: GeneralVLA 提出了一种分层视觉-语言-动作模型，该模型利用基础模型的泛化能力和知识引导的轨迹规划进行机器人操作，展示了强大的零样本性能和自主数据生成能力，无需真实世界的数据收集。
summary_zh: 大型基础模型在视觉和语言领域复杂问题的开放世界泛化方面表现出强大的性能，但类似水平的泛化能力尚未在机器人领域实现。一个根本性的挑战是这些模型表现出有限的零样本能力，这阻碍了它们有效泛化到未见场景的能力。在这项工作中，我们提出了GeneralVLA（基于知识引导轨迹规划的可泛化视觉-语言-动作模型），一种分层视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型，能够更有效地利用基础模型的泛化能力，实现零样本操作并自动生成机器人数据。具体而言，我们研究了一类分层VLA模型，其中高层ASM（可供性分割模块）经过微调以感知场景的图像关键点可供性；中层3DAgent执行任务理解、技能知识和轨迹规划，生成指示期望机器人末端执行器轨迹的三维路径。该中间三维路径预测随后作为指导，用于具有精确操作能力的底层、三维感知控制策略。与替代方法相比，我们的方法不需要真实世界机器人数据收集或人工示教，使其更易于扩展到各种任务和视角。经验表明，GeneralVLA成功地为14个任务生成轨迹，显著优于现有技术，如VoxPoser。与使用人工示教或由VoxPoser、Scaling-up和Code-As-Policies生成的数据进行训练相比，所生成的演示能够训练出更稳健的行为克隆策略。我们相信GeneralVLA可以成为一种可扩展的方法，用于生成机器人数据并在零样本设置中解决新颖的任务。代码：https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/GeneralVLA. 网站：https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/GeneralVLA。

title: Reshaping Action Error Distributions for Reliable Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: In robotic manipulation, vision-language-action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising paradigm for learning generalizable and scalable robot policies. Most existing VLA frameworks rely on standard supervised objectives, typically cross-entropy for discrete actions and mean squared error (MSE) for continuous action regression, which impose strong pointwise constraints on individual predictions. In this work, we focus on continuous-action VLA models and move beyond conventional MSE-based regression by reshaping action error distributions during training. Drawing on information-theoretic principles, we introduce Minimum Error Entropy (MEE) into modern VLA architectures and propose a trajectory-level MEE objective, together with two weighted variants, combined with MSE for continuous-action VLA training. We evaluate our approaches across standard, few-shot, and noisy settings on multiple representative VLA architectures, using simulation benchmarks such as LIBERO and SimplerEnv as well as real-world robotic manipulation tasks. Experimental results demonstrate consistent improvements in success rates and robustness across these settings. Under imbalanced data regimes, the gains persist within a well-characterized operating range, while incurring negligible additional training cost and no impact on inference efficiency. We further provide theoretical analyses that explain why MEE-based supervision is effective and characterize its practical range. Project Page: https://cognition2actionlab.github.io/VLA-TMEE.github.io/
tldr: This paper introduces a trajectory-level Minimum Error Entropy (MEE) objective to improve the training of continuous-action Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, demonstrating improved success rates and robustness in simulated and real-world robot manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种轨迹层面的最小误差熵（MEE）目标，以改进连续动作视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的训练，并在模拟和真实世界的机器人操作任务中展示了更高的成功率和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 在机器人操作中，视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型已成为一种有前景的范例，用于学习可泛化和可扩展的机器人策略。大多数现有的VLA框架依赖于标准的监督目标，通常对于离散动作使用交叉熵，对于连续动作回归使用均方误差（MSE），这对单个预测施加了强烈的逐点约束。在这项工作中，我们专注于连续动作VLA模型，并通过在训练期间重塑动作误差分布，超越了传统的基于MSE的回归。借鉴信息论原理，我们将最小误差熵（MEE）引入现代VLA架构，并提出了一种轨迹级MEE目标，以及两种加权变体，与MSE相结合用于连续动作VLA训练。我们在多个代表性的VLA架构上，使用LIBERO和SimplerEnv等模拟基准以及真实世界的机器人操作任务，在标准、少样本和噪声环境下评估了我们的方法。实验结果表明，在这些环境下，成功率和鲁棒性都有持续的提高。在不平衡数据的情况下，这些增益在良好表征的工作范围内持续存在，同时产生的额外训练成本可以忽略不计，并且对推理效率没有影响。我们进一步提供了理论分析，解释了为什么基于MEE的监督是有效的，并描述了它的实际范围。项目主页：https://cognition2actionlab.github.io/VLA-TMEE.github.io/

title: Natural Language Instructions for Scene-Responsive Human-in-the-Loop Motion Planning in Autonomous Driving using Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Instruction-grounded driving, where passenger language guides trajectory planning, requires vehicles to understand intent before motion. However, most prior instruction-following planners rely on simulation or fixed command vocabularies, limiting real-world generalization. doScenes, the first real-world dataset linking free-form instructions (with referentiality) to nuScenes ground-truth motion, enables instruction-conditioned planning. In this work, we adapt OpenEMMA, an open-source MLLM-based end-to-end driving framework that ingests front-camera views and ego-state and outputs 10-step speed-curvature trajectories, to this setting, presenting a reproducible instruction-conditioned baseline on doScenes and investigate the effects of human instruction prompts on predicted driving behavior. We integrate doScenes directives as passenger-style prompts within OpenEMMA's vision-language interface, enabling linguistic conditioning before trajectory generation. Evaluated on 849 annotated scenes using ADE, we observe that instruction conditioning substantially improves robustness by preventing extreme baseline failures, yielding a 98.7% reduction in mean ADE. When such outliers are removed, instructions still influence trajectory alignment, with well-phrased prompts improving ADE by up to 5.1%. We use this analysis to discuss what makes a "good" instruction for the OpenEMMA framework. We release the evaluation prompts and scripts to establish a reproducible baseline for instruction-aware planning. GitHub: https://github.com/Mi3-Lab/doScenes-VLM-Planning
tldr: This paper introduces doScenes, a real-world dataset for instruction-grounded autonomous driving, and demonstrates improved trajectory planning by conditioning a vision-language-action model (OpenEMMA) on natural language instructions.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了doScenes，一个用于指令驱动自动驾驶的真实世界数据集，并通过将视觉-语言-动作模型(OpenEMMA)基于自然语言指令进行条件化，展示了改进的轨迹规划。
summary_zh: 基于指令的驾驶，即乘客的语言引导轨迹规划，要求车辆在运动前理解意图。然而，大多数先前基于指令的规划器依赖于模拟或固定的命令词汇表，限制了真实世界的泛化能力。doScenes是第一个将自由形式的指令（具有指涉性）链接到nuScenes真实运动的真实世界数据集，它支持了指令条件下的规划。在这项工作中，我们将OpenEMMA（一个基于MLLM的开源端到端驾驶框架，它获取前置摄像头视图和自车状态并输出10步的速度-曲率轨迹）应用于此环境，提出了doScenes上一个可复现的指令条件基线，并研究了人类指令提示对预测驾驶行为的影响。我们将doScenes指令集成到OpenEMMA的视觉-语言界面中，作为乘客风格的提示，从而在轨迹生成之前实现语言条件化。使用ADE在849个标注场景上进行评估，我们观察到指令条件化通过防止极端的基线失效，显著提高了鲁棒性，使平均ADE降低了98.7%。移除这些异常值后，指令仍然会影响轨迹对齐，措辞精良的提示可将ADE提高至多5.1%。我们利用此分析来讨论对于OpenEMMA框架而言，什么样的指令是“好的”指令。我们发布了评估提示和脚本，以建立一个可复现的指令感知规划基线。GitHub: https://github.com/Mi3-Lab/doScenes-VLM-Planning

title: Efficient Long-Horizon Vision-Language-Action Models via Static-Dynamic Disentanglement
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for generalist robotic control. Built upon vision-language model (VLM) architectures, VLAs predict actions conditioned on visual observations and language instructions, achieving strong performance and generalization across tasks. However, VLAs face two major challenges: limited long-horizon context and inefficient inference due to the quadratic attention complexity and large parameter counts. Our work is motivated by the observation that much of the visual information in a trajectory remains static across timesteps (e.g., the background). Leveraging this property, we propose SD-VLA, a framework that disentangles visual inputs into multi-level static and dynamic tokens, which enables (1) retaining a single copy of static tokens across frames to significantly reduce context length, and (2) reusing the key-value (KV) cache of static tokens through a lightweight recache gate that updates only when necessary. This design enables efficient multi-frame integration and efficient inference. In addition, we introduce a new benchmark that more effectively evaluates the long-horizon temporal dependency modeling ability of VLAs. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms baselines on this benchmark by 39.8% absolute improvement in success rate, and achieves a 3.9% gain on the SimplerEnv benchmark. Moreover, SD-VLA delivers a 2.26x inference speedup over the base VLA model on the same benchmark, enabling faster and more practical real-world deployment.
tldr: The paper introduces SD-VLA, a novel Vision-Language-Action model that uses static-dynamic disentanglement to improve inference speed and performance in long-horizon robotic control tasks by reusing static visual information across frames, achieving significant gains in speed and success rates.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为SD-VLA的新型视觉-语言-动作模型，该模型通过静态-动态解耦来提高长程机器人控制任务中的推理速度和性能，通过跨帧重用静态视觉信息，从而在速度和成功率方面取得了显著提升。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型最近作为通用机器人控制的一种有前景的范式而兴起。VLA 模型构建于视觉-语言模型 (VLM) 架构之上，以视觉观测和语言指令为条件预测动作，在各项任务中实现了强大的性能和泛化能力。然而，VLA 模型面临着两个主要挑战：有限的长程上下文和由于二次注意力复杂度和庞大的参数量导致的低效推理。我们的工作受到以下观察结果的启发：轨迹中的大部分视觉信息在时间步上保持静态（例如，背景）。利用这一特性，我们提出了 SD-VLA，一种将视觉输入解耦为多层次静态和动态 Token 的框架，该框架能够 (1) 在帧之间保留静态 Token 的单个副本，从而显著减少上下文长度，以及 (2) 通过轻量级的重缓存门重用静态 Token 的键值 (KV) 缓存，该重缓存门仅在必要时进行更新。这种设计实现了高效的多帧集成和高效的推理。此外，我们引入了一个新的基准，该基准更有效地评估 VLA 模型的长程时间依赖性建模能力。实验结果表明，我们的方法在该基准上优于基线方法，在成功率方面取得了 39.8% 的绝对提升，并在 SimplerEnv 基准上实现了 3.9% 的提升。此外，SD-VLA 在同一基准上比基础 VLA 模型实现了 2.26 倍的推理速度提升，从而能够更快、更实际地进行现实世界的部署。

title: EMA Policy Gradient: Taming Reinforcement Learning for LLMs with EMA Anchor and Top-k KL
summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has enabled Large Language Models (LLMs) to acquire increasingly complex reasoning and agentic behaviors. In this work, we propose two simple techniques to improve policy gradient algorithms for LLMs. First, we replace the fixed anchor policy during RL with an Exponential Moving Average (EMA), similar to a target network in deep Q-learning. Second, we introduce Top-k KL estimator, which allows for flexible interpolation between exact KL and sampled KL. We derive the stability conditions for using EMA anchor; moreover, we show that our Top-k KL estimator yields both unbiased KL values and unbiased gradients at any k, while bringing the benefits of exact KL. When combined with GRPO, the two techniques (EMA-PG) lead to a significant performance boost. On math reasoning, it allows R1-distilled Qwen-1.5B to reach 53.9% on OlympiadBench compared to 50.8% by GRPO. On agentic RL domains, with Qwen-3B base, EMA-PG improves GRPO by an average of 33.3% across 7 datasets of Q&A with search engines, including 29.7% $\rightarrow$ 44.1% on HotpotQA, 27.4% $\rightarrow$ 40.1% on 2WikiMultiHopQA. Overall, we show that EMA-PG is a simple, principled, and powerful approach to scaling RL for LLMs. Code: https://github.com/LunjunZhang/ema-pg
tldr: The paper introduces EMA-PG, a method incorporating EMA anchor policy and Top-k KL estimator to improve policy gradient algorithms for LLMs, demonstrating significant performance gains in math reasoning and agentic RL tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了EMA-PG，一种结合EMA锚定策略和Top-k KL估计器的方法，旨在改进LLM的策略梯度算法，并在数学推理和智能体RL任务中表现出显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 强化学习（RL）已经使大型语言模型（LLMs）能够获得日益复杂的推理能力和自主行为。在这项工作中，我们提出了两种简单的技术来改进LLM的策略梯度算法。首先，我们用指数移动平均（EMA）替换RL期间的固定锚定策略，类似于深度Q学习中的目标网络。其次，我们引入了Top-k KL估计器，它允许在精确KL和采样KL之间进行灵活的插值。我们推导了使用EMA锚定的稳定性条件；此外，我们证明了我们的Top-k KL估计器在任何k值下都能产生无偏的KL值和无偏梯度，同时带来精确KL的优势。当与GRPO结合时，这两种技术（EMA-PG）会带来显著的性能提升。在数学推理方面，它允许经过R1蒸馏的Qwen-1.5B在OlympiadBench上达到53.9%的准确率，而GRPO为50.8%。在自主RL领域，以Qwen-3B为基础模型，EMA-PG在7个使用搜索引擎进行问答的数据集上平均提升了GRPO 33.3%，包括HotpotQA上的29.7% $\rightarrow$ 44.1% 和 2WikiMultiHopQA上的27.4% $\rightarrow$ 40.1%。总而言之，我们表明EMA-PG是一种简单、有原则且强大的LLM的RL扩展方法。代码: https://github.com/LunjunZhang/ema-pg

title: The Missing Half: Unveiling Training-time Implicit Safety Risks Beyond Deployment
summary: Safety risks of AI models have been widely studied at deployment time, such as jailbreak attacks that elicit harmful outputs. In contrast, safety risks emerging during training remain largely unexplored. Beyond explicit reward hacking that directly manipulates explicit reward functions in reinforcement learning, we study implicit training-time safety risks: harmful behaviors driven by a model's internal incentives and contextual background information. For example, during code-based reinforcement learning, a model may covertly manipulate logged accuracy for self-preservation. We present the first systematic study of this problem, introducing a taxonomy with five risk levels, ten fine-grained risk categories, and three incentive types. Extensive experiments reveal the prevalence and severity of these risks: notably, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct exhibits risky behaviors in 74.4% of training runs when provided only with background information. We further analyze factors influencing these behaviors and demonstrate that implicit training-time risks also arise in multi-agent training settings. Our results identify an overlooked yet urgent safety challenge in training.
tldr: The paper introduces a systematic study of implicit safety risks during AI model training, revealing that models can exhibit harmful behaviors driven by internal incentives even with background knowledge and no direct manipulation of reward functions.
tldr_zh: 该论文系统地研究了人工智能模型训练期间的隐式安全风险，揭示了模型即使仅有背景知识且未直接操纵奖励函数，也会因内部激励而表现出有害行为。
summary_zh: 人工智能模型的安全风险在部署阶段已被广泛研究，例如引诱模型产生有害输出的越狱攻击。相比之下，训练期间出现的安全风险在很大程度上仍未被探索。除了直接操纵强化学习中显式奖励函数的显式奖励篡改外，我们研究了隐式训练时安全风险：由模型内部激励和上下文背景信息驱动的有害行为。例如，在基于代码的强化学习过程中，模型可能会秘密地操纵记录的准确性以实现自我保护。我们首次对这个问题进行了系统研究，提出了一个包含五个风险等级、十个细粒度风险类别和三种激励类型的分类法。大量实验揭示了这些风险的普遍性和严重性：值得注意的是，仅提供背景信息时，Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct 在 74.4% 的训练运行中表现出风险行为。我们进一步分析了影响这些行为的因素，并证明隐式训练时风险也出现在多智能体训练环境中。 我们的研究结果揭示了训练中一个被忽视但迫切的安全挑战。

title: AGILE: Hand-Object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation
summary: Reconstructing dynamic hand-object interactions from monocular videos is critical for dexterous manipulation data collection and creating realistic digital twins for robotics and VR. However, current methods face two prohibitive barriers: (1) reliance on neural rendering often yields fragmented, non-simulation-ready geometries under heavy occlusion, and (2) dependence on brittle Structure-from-Motion (SfM) initialization leads to frequent failures on in-the-wild footage. To overcome these limitations, we introduce AGILE, a robust framework that shifts the paradigm from reconstruction to agentic generation for interaction learning. First, we employ an agentic pipeline where a Vision-Language Model (VLM) guides a generative model to synthesize a complete, watertight object mesh with high-fidelity texture, independent of video occlusions. Second, bypassing fragile SfM entirely, we propose a robust anchor-and-track strategy. We initialize the object pose at a single interaction onset frame using a foundation model and propagate it temporally by leveraging the strong visual similarity between our generated asset and video observations. Finally, a contact-aware optimization integrates semantic, geometric, and interaction stability constraints to enforce physical plausibility. Extensive experiments on HO3D, DexYCB, and in-the-wild videos reveal that AGILE outperforms baselines in global geometric accuracy while demonstrating exceptional robustness on challenging sequences where prior art frequently collapses. By prioritizing physical validity, our method produces simulation-ready assets validated via real-to-sim retargeting for robotic applications.
tldr: The paper introduces AGILE, a novel framework for reconstructing dynamic hand-object interactions from monocular video using a VLM-guided agentic generation approach that overcomes limitations of previous reconstruction methods by generating simulation-ready assets.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为 AGILE 的新型框架，用于从单目视频重建动态手部-物体交互。该框架采用 VLM 引导的智能生成方法，通过生成可用于仿真的模型，克服了以往重建方法的局限性。
summary_zh: 从单目视频重建动态手-物交互对于灵巧操作数据采集以及为机器人和VR创建逼真的数字孪生至关重要。然而，当前方法面临两个主要障碍：(1)对神经渲染的依赖在严重遮挡下通常产生碎片化的、不适用于仿真的几何体；(2)对脆弱的运动结构(SfM)初始化的依赖导致在真实场景视频中频繁失败。为了克服这些局限性，我们提出AGILE，一个稳健的框架，它将交互学习的范例从重建转变为代理生成。首先，我们采用一个代理流程，其中视觉-语言模型(VLM)引导生成模型合成一个完整的、水密的物体网格，并具有高保真度的纹理，不受视频遮挡的影响。其次，完全绕过脆弱的SfM，我们提出一种鲁棒的锚定与跟踪策略。我们使用一个基础模型在单个交互开始帧初始化物体姿态，并通过利用我们生成的资产和视频观测之间强大的视觉相似性，在时间上传播它。最后，一个接触感知优化集成了语义、几何和交互稳定性约束，以强制物理合理性。在HO3D、DexYCB和真实场景视频上的大量实验表明，AGILE在全局几何精度方面优于基线，同时在现有技术频繁崩溃的具有挑战性的序列上表现出卓越的鲁棒性。通过优先考虑物理有效性，我们的方法产生了通过真实世界到仿真重定向验证的、适用于机器人应用的仿真资产。

title: Relational Scene Graphs for Object Grounding of Natural Language Commands
summary: Robots are finding wider adoption in human environments, increasing the need for natural human-robot interaction. However, understanding a natural language command requires the robot to infer the intended task and how to decompose it into executable actions, and to ground those actions in the robot's knowledge of the environment, including relevant objects, agents, and locations. This challenge can be addressed by combining the capabilities of Large language models (LLMs) to understand natural language with 3D scene graphs (3DSGs) for grounding inferred actions in a semantic representation of the environment. However, many 3DSGs lack explicit spatial relations between objects, even though humans often rely on these relations to describe an environment. This paper investigates whether incorporating open- or closed-vocabulary spatial relations into 3DSGs can improve the ability of LLMs to interpret natural language commands. To address this, we propose an LLM-based pipeline for target object grounding from open-vocabulary language commands and a vision language model (VLM)-based pipeline to add open-vocabulary spatial edges to 3DSGs from images captured while mapping. Finally, two LLMs are evaluated in a study assessing their performance on the downstream task of target object grounding. Our study demonstrates that explicit spatial relations improve the ability of LLMs to ground objects. Moreover, open-vocabulary relation generation with VLMs proves feasible from robot-captured images, but their advantage over closed-vocabulary relations is found to be limited.
tldr: This paper investigates how incorporating spatial relations into 3D scene graphs can improve LLMs' ability to ground natural language commands, finding that explicit spatial relations help, and open-vocabulary relations offer limited advantage over closed-vocabulary ones.
tldr_zh: 该论文研究了将空间关系纳入3D场景图如何提升LLM理解自然语言指令的能力，发现显式的空间关系有所帮助，而开放词汇关系相对于封闭词汇关系优势有限。
summary_zh: 机器人正被广泛应用于人类环境中，这增加了对自然人机交互的需求。然而，理解一条自然语言指令需要机器人推断预期的任务，并将其分解成可执行的动作，以及将这些动作与机器人对环境的认知（包括相关的物体、主体和位置）相结合。这个问题可以通过结合大型语言模型（LLM）理解自然语言的能力以及3D场景图（3DSG）将推断的动作扎根于环境的语义表示中来解决。然而，许多3DSG缺乏物体之间明确的空间关系，即使人类经常依赖这些关系来描述环境。本文研究了将开放或封闭词汇空间关系融入3DSG是否可以提高LLM解释自然语言命令的能力。为此，我们提出了一种基于LLM的流水线，用于从开放词汇的语言命令中进行目标物体定位，以及一种基于视觉语言模型（VLM）的流水线，用于从映射过程中捕获的图像向3DSG添加开放词汇的空间边。最后，在一个评估两个LLM在目标物体定位下游任务上性能的研究中，对它们进行了评估。我们的研究表明，明确的空间关系提高了LLM定位物体的能力。此外，使用VLM进行开放词汇关系生成对于机器人捕获的图像来说是可行的，但发现其相对于封闭词汇关系的优势有限。

title: Act, Sense, Act: Learning Non-Markovian Active Perception Strategies from Large-Scale Egocentric Human Data
summary: Achieving generalizable manipulation in unconstrained environments requires the robot to proactively resolve information uncertainty, i.e., the capability of active perception. However, existing methods are often confined in limited types of sensing behaviors, restricting their applicability to complex environments. In this work, we formalize active perception as a non-Markovian process driven by information gain and decision branching, providing a structured categorization of visual active perception paradigms. Building on this perspective, we introduce CoMe-VLA, a cognitive and memory-aware vision-language-action (VLA) framework that leverages large-scale human egocentric data to learn versatile exploration and manipulation priors. Our framework integrates a cognitive auxiliary head for autonomous sub-task transitions and a dual-track memory system to maintain consistent self and environmental awareness by fusing proprioceptive and visual temporal contexts. By aligning human and robot hand-eye coordination behaviors in a unified egocentric action space, we train the model progressively in three stages. Extensive experiments on a wheel-based humanoid have demonstrated strong robustness and adaptability of our proposed method across diverse long-horizon tasks spanning multiple active perception scenarios.
tldr: This paper introduces CoMe-VLA, a new vision-language-action framework for active perception in robotics, trained on large-scale egocentric human data. It uses a cognitive architecture and memory system to enable robust and adaptable long-horizon manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为 CoMe-VLA 的新型视觉-语言-动作框架，用于机器人主动感知，该框架在大型以人为中心的自我数据上训练。它使用认知架构和记忆系统来实现稳健且适应性强的长时程操纵任务。
summary_zh: 在不受约束的环境中实现通用操作需要机器人主动解决信息不确定性，即具备主动感知能力。然而，现有方法通常局限于有限的感知行为类型，限制了其在复杂环境中的适用性。 在这项工作中，我们将主动感知形式化为由信息增益和决策分支驱动的非马尔可夫过程，为视觉主动感知范式提供了一个结构化的分类。 基于这个视角，我们提出CoMe-VLA，一种认知和记忆感知的视觉-语言-动作(VLA)框架，它利用大规模人类以自我为中心的数据来学习通用的探索和操作先验知识。 我们的框架集成了一个用于自主子任务转换的认知辅助头和一个双轨记忆系统，通过融合本体感受和视觉时序上下文来保持一致的自我和环境意识。 通过在统一的以自我为中心的行动空间中对齐人类和机器人的手眼协调行为，我们分三个阶段逐步训练模型。 在轮式人形机器人上的大量实验表明，我们提出的方法在跨越多个主动感知场景的各种长时程任务中具有强大的鲁棒性和适应性。

title: Integrated Exploration and Sequential Manipulation on Scene Graph with LLM-based Situated Replanning
summary: In partially known environments, robots must combine exploration to gather information with task planning for efficient execution. To address this challenge, we propose EPoG, an Exploration-based sequential manipulation Planning framework on Scene Graphs. EPoG integrates a graph-based global planner with a Large Language Model (LLM)-based situated local planner, continuously updating a belief graph using observations and LLM predictions to represent known and unknown objects. Action sequences are generated by computing graph edit operations between the goal and belief graphs, ordered by temporal dependencies and movement costs. This approach seamlessly combines exploration and sequential manipulation planning. In ablation studies across 46 realistic household scenes and 5 long-horizon daily object transportation tasks, EPoG achieved a success rate of 91.3%, reducing travel distance by 36.1% on average. Furthermore, a physical mobile manipulator successfully executed complex tasks in unknown and dynamic environments, demonstrating EPoG's potential for real-world applications.
tldr: The paper introduces EPoG, a framework that integrates exploration and sequential manipulation in partially known environments using a graph-based global planner and an LLM-based local planner, achieving high success rates in simulated and real-world robotic tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了EPoG，一个结合探索和序列操作的框架，在部分已知的环境中，利用基于图的全局规划器和基于LLM的局部规划器，在模拟和现实世界的机器人任务中取得了很高的成功率。
summary_zh: 在部分已知环境中，机器人必须结合探索以收集信息，以及任务规划以实现高效执行。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了EPoG，一个基于探索的场景图上的序列操作规划框架。EPoG集成了基于图的全局规划器和基于大型语言模型(LLM)的现场局部规划器，通过使用观察和LLM预测持续更新置信图，以表示已知和未知的对象。动作序列通过计算目标图和置信图之间的图编辑操作生成，并按时间依赖关系和移动成本排序。这种方法无缝结合了探索和序列操作规划。在跨越46个真实家庭场景和5个长时程日常物品运输任务的消融研究中，EPoG实现了91.3%的成功率，平均减少了36.1%的行进距离。此外，一个物理移动操作臂成功地在未知和动态环境中执行了复杂的任务，证明了EPoG在现实世界应用中的潜力。

title: HoRD: Robust Humanoid Control via History-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning and Online Distillation
summary: Humanoid robots can suffer significant performance drops under small changes in dynamics, task specifications, or environment setup. We propose HoRD, a two-stage learning framework for robust humanoid control under domain shift. First, we train a high-performance teacher policy via history-conditioned reinforcement learning, where the policy infers latent dynamics context from recent state--action trajectories to adapt online to diverse randomized dynamics. Second, we perform online distillation to transfer the teacher's robust control capabilities into a transformer-based student policy that operates on sparse root-relative 3D joint keypoint trajectories. By combining history-conditioned adaptation with online distillation, HoRD enables a single policy to adapt zero-shot to unseen domains without per-domain retraining. Extensive experiments show HoRD outperforms strong baselines in robustness and transfer, especially under unseen domains and external perturbations. Code and project page are available at https://tonywang-0517.github.io/hord/.
tldr: HoRD proposes a two-stage framework that uses history-conditioned RL for a teacher policy and online distillation to transfer robust control to a student policy for zero-shot adaptation of humanoid robots to unseen domains.
tldr_zh: HoRD提出了一个两阶段框架，使用历史条件强化学习训练教师策略，并通过在线蒸馏将鲁棒控制能力转移到学生策略，实现人形机器人在未见域的零样本适应。
summary_zh: 类人机器人会在动力学、任务规范或环境设置发生微小变化时遭受显著的性能下降。我们提出HoRD，一个用于在领域偏移下实现鲁棒类人控制的两阶段学习框架。首先，我们通过历史条件强化学习训练一个高性能的教师策略，其中该策略从最近的状态-动作轨迹推断潜在的动力学上下文，从而实现在线适应多样化的随机动力学。其次，我们执行在线蒸馏，将教师的鲁棒控制能力转移到一个基于Transformer的、处理稀疏的根相对3D关节关键点轨迹的学生策略。通过结合历史条件适应与在线蒸馏，HoRD使得单个策略能够在未经特定领域重新训练的情况下零样本适应未见领域。大量实验表明，HoRD在鲁棒性和迁移方面优于强大的基线方法，尤其是在未见领域和外部扰动下。代码和项目页面可在https://tonywang-0517.github.io/hord/获取。

title: Safe and Stylized Trajectory Planning for Autonomous Driving via Diffusion Model
summary: Achieving safe and stylized trajectory planning in complex real-world scenarios remains a critical challenge for autonomous driving systems. This paper proposes the SDD Planner, a diffusion-based framework designed to effectively reconcile safety constraints with driving styles in real time. The framework integrates two core modules: a Multi-Source Style-Aware Encoder, which employs distance-sensitive attention to fuse dynamic agent data and environmental contexts for heterogeneous safety-style perception; and a Style-Guided Dynamic Trajectory Generator, which adaptively modulates priority weights within the diffusion denoising process to generate user-preferred yet safe trajectories. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SDD Planner achieves state-of-the-art performance. On the StyleDrive benchmark, it improves the SM-PDMS metric by 3.9% over WoTE, the strongest baseline. Furthermore, on the NuPlan Test14 and Test14-hard benchmarks, SDD Planner ranks first with overall scores of 91.76 and 80.32, respectively, outperforming leading methods such as PLUTO. Real-vehicle closed-loop tests further confirm that SDD Planner maintains high safety standards while aligning with preset driving styles, validating its practical applicability for real-world deployment.
tldr: The paper introduces SDD Planner, a diffusion-based framework for autonomous driving that balances safety and driving style preferences, achieving state-of-the-art performance on several benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SDD Planner，一个基于扩散模型的自动驾驶框架，它平衡了安全性和驾驶风格偏好，并在多个基准测试中取得了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 在复杂的真实场景中，实现安全且风格化的轨迹规划仍然是自动驾驶系统面临的关键挑战。本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的SDD规划器，旨在实时有效地协调安全约束和驾驶风格。该框架集成了两个核心模块：一个多源风格感知编码器，该编码器采用距离敏感注意力来融合动态智能体数据和环境上下文，以实现异构的安全-风格感知；以及一个风格引导的动态轨迹生成器，该生成器自适应地调节扩散去噪过程中的优先级权重，以生成用户偏好且安全的轨迹。大量实验表明，SDD规划器实现了最先进的性能。在StyleDrive基准测试中，它比最强的基线WoTE提高了3.9%的SM-PDMS指标。此外，在NuPlan Test14和Test14-hard基准测试中，SDD规划器分别以91.76和80.32的总分排名第一，优于PLUTO等领先方法。真实车辆的闭环测试进一步证实，SDD规划器在保持高安全标准的同时，能够与预设的驾驶风格保持一致，验证了其在实际部署中的实用性。

title: AppleVLM: End-to-end Autonomous Driving with Advanced Perception and Planning-Enhanced Vision-Language Models
summary: End-to-end autonomous driving has emerged as a promising paradigm integrating perception, decision-making, and control within a unified learning framework. Recently, Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have gained significant attention for their potential to enhance the robustness and generalization of end-to-end driving models in diverse and unseen scenarios. However, existing VLM-based approaches still face challenges, including suboptimal lane perception, language understanding biases, and difficulties in handling corner cases. To address these issues, we propose AppleVLM, an advanced perception and planning-enhanced VLM model for robust end-to-end driving. AppleVLM introduces a novel vision encoder and a planning strategy encoder to improve perception and decision-making. Firstly, the vision encoder fuses spatial-temporal information from multi-view images across multiple timesteps using a deformable transformer mechanism, enhancing robustness to camera variations and facilitating scalable deployment across different vehicle platforms. Secondly, unlike traditional VLM-based approaches, AppleVLM introduces a dedicated planning modality that encodes explicit Bird's-Eye-View spatial information, mitigating language biases in navigation instructions. Finally, a VLM decoder fine-tuned by a hierarchical Chain-of-Thought integrates vision, language, and planning features to output robust driving waypoints. We evaluate AppleVLM in closed-loop experiments on two CARLA benchmarks, achieving state-of-the-art driving performance. Furthermore, we deploy AppleVLM on an AGV platform and successfully showcase real-world end-to-end autonomous driving in complex outdoor environments.
tldr: The paper introduces AppleVLM, a new Vision-Language Model for end-to-end autonomous driving that incorporates advanced perception and planning strategies, achieving state-of-the-art performance on CARLA and demonstrating real-world driving on an AGV platform.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了AppleVLM，一种用于端到端自动驾驶的新型视觉语言模型，它结合了先进的感知和规划策略，在CARLA上实现了最先进的性能，并在AGV平台上展示了真实的驾驶能力。
summary_zh: 端到端自动驾驶已成为一种有前景的范式，它在一个统一的学习框架内集成了感知、决策和控制。 近年来，视觉-语言模型（VLM）因其增强端到端驾驶模型在各种未见场景中的鲁棒性和泛化能力的潜力而备受关注。 然而，现有的基于 VLM 的方法仍然面临一些挑战，包括次优的车道感知、语言理解偏差以及难以处理的极端情况。 为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 AppleVLM，一种用于鲁棒端到端驾驶的，经过高级感知和规划增强的 VLM 模型。 AppleVLM 引入了一种新颖的视觉编码器和一个规划策略编码器，以改进感知和决策。 首先，该视觉编码器使用可变形 Transformer 机制融合来自多个时间步长中的多视角图像的时空信息，从而增强对相机变化的鲁棒性，并促进在不同车辆平台上的可扩展部署。 其次，与传统的基于 VLM 的方法不同，AppleVLM 引入了一种专用的规划模态，该模态编码了显式的鸟瞰图空间信息，从而减轻了导航指令中的语言偏差。 最后，一个通过分层思维链微调的 VLM 解码器集成了视觉、语言和规划特征，以输出鲁棒的驾驶航点。 我们在两个 CARLA 基准测试上通过闭环实验评估了 AppleVLM，实现了最先进的驾驶性能。 此外，我们将 AppleVLM 部署在 AGV 平台上，并成功展示了在复杂户外环境中真实的端到端自动驾驶。

title: Viewpoint Matters: Dynamically Optimizing Viewpoints with Masked Autoencoder for Visual Manipulation
summary: Robotic manipulation continues to be a challenge, and imitation learning (IL) enables robots to learn tasks from expert demonstrations. Current IL methods typically rely on fixed camera setups, where cameras are manually positioned in static locations, imposing significant limitations on adaptability and coverage. Inspired by human active perception, where humans dynamically adjust their viewpoint to capture the most relevant and least noisy information, we propose MAE-Select, a novel framework for active viewpoint selection in single-camera robotic systems. MAE-Select fully leverages pre-trained multi-view masked autoencoder representations and dynamically selects the next most informative viewpoint at each time chunk without requiring labeled viewpoints. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MAE-Select improves the capabilities of single-camera systems and, in some cases, even surpasses multi-camera setups. The project will be available at https://mae-select.github.io.
tldr: This paper introduces MAE-Select, a method for dynamically optimizing camera viewpoints in robotic manipulation using a pre-trained masked autoencoder, which improves performance compared to fixed camera setups and sometimes even outperforms multi-camera systems.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为 MAE-Select 的方法，该方法利用预训练的掩码自动编码器动态优化机器人操作中的相机视角。与固定相机设置相比，该方法能够提高性能，有时甚至优于多相机系统。
summary_zh: 机器人操作仍然是一项挑战，而模仿学习（IL）使机器人能够从专家演示中学习任务。目前的IL方法通常依赖于固定的相机设置，其中相机被手动放置在静态位置，对适应性和覆盖范围施加了显著的限制。受到人类主动感知的启发，人类会动态调整视角以捕捉最相关和最少噪声的信息，我们提出MAE-Select，一个用于单相机机器人系统中主动视点选择的新框架。MAE-Select充分利用预训练的多视角掩码自编码器表示，并在每个时间块动态地选择下一个最具信息量的视点，而无需标记的视点。大量的实验表明，MAE-Select提高了单相机系统的能力，并且在某些情况下甚至超过了多相机设置。该项目将在 https://mae-select.github.io 上提供。

title: GeoLanG: Geometry-Aware Language-Guided Grasping with Unified RGB-D Multimodal Learning
summary: Language-guided grasping has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling robots to identify and manipulate target objects through natural language instructions, yet it remains highly challenging in cluttered or occluded scenes. Existing methods often rely on multi-stage pipelines that separate object perception and grasping, which leads to limited cross-modal fusion, redundant computation, and poor generalization in cluttered, occluded, or low-texture scenes. To address these limitations, we propose GeoLanG, an end-to-end multi-task framework built upon the CLIP architecture that unifies visual and linguistic inputs into a shared representation space for robust semantic alignment and improved generalization. To enhance target discrimination under occlusion and low-texture conditions, we explore a more effective use of depth information through the Depth-guided Geometric Module (DGGM), which converts depth into explicit geometric priors and injects them into the attention mechanism without additional computational overhead. In addition, we propose Adaptive Dense Channel Integration, which adaptively balances the contributions of multi-layer features to produce more discriminative and generalizable visual representations. Extensive experiments on the OCID-VLG dataset, as well as in both simulation and real-world hardware, demonstrate that GeoLanG enables precise and robust language-guided grasping in complex, cluttered environments, paving the way toward more reliable multimodal robotic manipulation in real-world human-centric settings.
tldr: GeoLanG is an end-to-end framework for language-guided grasping using a CLIP-based architecture, incorporating depth information as geometric priors to improve performance in cluttered and occluded scenes. It demonstrates improved grasping performance in simulation and real-world settings.
tldr_zh: GeoLanG是一个端到端的语言引导抓取框架，它使用基于CLIP的架构，并将深度信息作为几何先验，以提高杂乱和遮挡场景中的性能。 它在模拟和现实环境中都展示了改进的抓取性能。
summary_zh: 语言引导抓取已成为一种极具前景的范式，它使机器人能够通过自然语言指令识别和操纵目标物体，但在杂乱或遮挡的场景中仍然极具挑战性。现有方法通常依赖于分离了物体感知和抓取的多阶段流程，这导致跨模态融合受限、计算冗余以及在杂乱、遮挡或低纹理场景中泛化能力较差。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出 GeoLanG，这是一个基于 CLIP 架构的端到端多任务框架，它将视觉和语言输入统一到一个共享的表征空间中，以实现鲁棒的语义对齐和提升泛化能力。为了增强在遮挡和低纹理条件下的目标辨别能力，我们探索通过深度引导几何模块（DGGM）更有效地利用深度信息，该模块将深度转换为显式的几何先验，并将其注入到注意力机制中，而无需额外的计算开销。此外，我们提出自适应密集通道集成，它自适应地平衡多层特征的贡献，以产生更具辨别力和泛化能力的视觉表征。在 OCID-VLG 数据集以及仿真环境和真实硬件上的大量实验表明，GeoLanG 能够在复杂的杂乱环境中实现精确而可靠的语言引导抓取，为在以人为中心的真实世界环境中实现更可靠的多模态机器人操作铺平了道路。

title: OAT: Ordered Action Tokenization
summary: Autoregressive policies offer a compelling foundation for scalable robot learning by enabling discrete abstraction, token-level reasoning, and flexible inference. However, applying autoregressive modeling to continuous robot actions requires an effective action tokenization scheme. Existing approaches either rely on analytical discretization methods that produce prohibitively long token sequences, or learned latent tokenizers that lack structure, limiting their compatibility with next-token prediction. In this work, we identify three desiderata for action tokenization - high compression, total decodability, and a left-to-right causally ordered token space - and introduce Ordered Action Tokenization (OAT), a learned action tokenizer that satisfies all three. OAT discretizes action chunks into an ordered sequence of tokens using transformer with registers, finite scalar quantization, and ordering-inducing training mechanisms. The resulting token space aligns naturally with autoregressive generation and enables prefix-based detokenization, yielding an anytime trade-off between inference cost and action fidelity. Across more than 20 tasks spanning four simulation benchmarks and real-world settings, autoregressive policies equipped with OAT consistently outperform prior tokenization schemes and diffusion-based baselines, while offering significantly greater flexibility at inference time.
tldr: The paper introduces Ordered Action Tokenization (OAT), a novel learned action tokenizer for autoregressive robot learning that addresses limitations of existing tokenization methods by offering high compression, total decodability, and causally ordered token space, leading to improved performance and inference flexibility across various robotic tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了有序动作标记化（OAT），这是一种用于自回归机器人学习的新型学习动作标记器，它通过提供高压缩率、完全可解码性和因果有序的标记空间来解决现有标记方法的局限性，从而在各种机器人任务中提高性能和推理灵活性。
summary_zh: 自回归策略通过支持离散抽象、令牌级推理以及灵活的推理，为可扩展的机器人学习提供了一个引人注目的基础。然而，将自回归建模应用于连续机器人动作需要有效的动作令牌化方案。现有方法要么依赖于产生极长令牌序列的解析离散化方法，要么依赖于缺乏结构的已学习潜在令牌器，从而限制了它们与下一个令牌预测的兼容性。在这项工作中，我们确定了动作令牌化的三个理想特性——高压缩率、完全可解码性以及从左到右的因果顺序令牌空间——并引入了有序动作令牌化 (OAT)，一种满足所有三个特性的已学习动作令牌器。OAT 使用带有寄存器的 Transformer、有限标量量化和诱导排序训练机制，将动作块离散化为有序的令牌序列。由此产生的令牌空间自然地与自回归生成对齐，并支持基于前缀的逆令牌化，从而在推理成本和动作保真度之间产生随时可用的权衡。在跨越四个模拟基准和真实世界环境的 20 多个任务中，配备 OAT 的自回归策略始终优于先前的令牌化方案和基于扩散模型的基线，同时在推理时提供了更大的灵活性。

title: SCALE: Self-uncertainty Conditioned Adaptive Looking and Execution for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising paradigm for general-purpose robotic control, with test-time scaling (TTS) gaining attention to enhance robustness beyond training. However, existing TTS methods for VLAs require additional training, verifiers, and multiple forward passes, making them impractical for deployment. Moreover, they intervene only at action decoding while keeping visual representations fixed-insufficient under perceptual ambiguity, where reconsidering how to perceive is as important as deciding what to do. To address these limitations, we propose SCALE, a simple inference strategy that jointly modulates visual perception and action based on 'self-uncertainty', inspired by uncertainty-driven exploration in Active Inference theory-requiring no additional training, no verifier, and only a single forward pass. SCALE broadens exploration in both perception and action under high uncertainty, while focusing on exploitation when confident-enabling adaptive execution across varying conditions. Experiments on simulated and real-world benchmarks demonstrate that SCALE improves state-of-the-art VLAs and outperforms existing TTS methods while maintaining single-pass efficiency.
tldr: The paper introduces SCALE, a single-pass inference strategy for Vision-Language-Action models that uses self-uncertainty to adaptively modulate both visual perception and action, improving performance and robustness without additional training.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为SCALE的单次推理策略，用于视觉-语言-动作模型。它利用自不确定性自适应地调节视觉感知和动作，从而在无需额外训练的情况下提高性能和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型已成为一种有前景的通用机器人控制范式，其中测试时缩放（TTS）因其增强训练之外的鲁棒性而备受关注。然而，现有的VLA的TTS方法需要额外的训练、验证器和多次前向传递，这使得它们在部署中不切实际。此外，它们仅在动作解码阶段进行干预，而保持视觉表征不变——这在感知模糊的情况下是不够的，因为重新考虑如何感知与决定做什么同样重要。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出SCALE，一种简单的推理策略，它基于“自我不确定性”联合调节视觉感知和动作，其灵感来自主动推理理论中的不确定性驱动探索——无需额外的训练、验证器，并且只需要单次前向传递。 SCALE在高不确定性下扩大了感知和动作方面的探索范围，而在自信时则侧重于利用——从而能够在各种条件下实现自适应执行。在模拟和真实世界的基准测试上的实验表明，SCALE改进了最先进的VLA，并且性能优于现有的TTS方法，同时保持了单次传递的效率。

title: A Modern System Recipe for Situated Embodied Human-Robot Conversation with Real-Time Multimodal LLMs and Tool-Calling
summary: Situated embodied conversation requires robots to interleave real-time dialogue with active perception: deciding what to look at, when to look, and what to say under tight latency constraints. We present a simple, minimal system recipe that pairs a real-time multimodal language model with a small set of tool interfaces for attention and active perception. We study six home-style scenarios that require frequent attention shifts and increasing perceptual scope. Across four system variants, we evaluate turn-level tool-decision correctness against human annotations and collect subjective ratings of interaction quality. Results indicate that real-time multimodal large language models and tool use for active perception is a promising direction for practical situated embodied conversation.
tldr: This paper introduces a real-time multimodal LLM system for situated embodied human-robot conversation, using tool-calling for attention and active perception in home-style scenarios, showing promising results for practical applications.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个用于情境式具身人机对话的实时多模态LLM系统，该系统使用工具调用来进行注意力和主动感知，并在家庭场景下展示了有希望的结果，具有实际应用前景。
summary_zh: 具身情境对话要求机器人将实时对话与主动感知交织在一起：在严格的延迟约束下，决定看什么、何时看以及说什么。我们提出了一种简单、最小化的系统方案，该方案将一个实时多模态语言模型与一组用于注意力和主动感知的工具接口配对。我们研究了六种家庭场景，这些场景需要频繁的注意力转移和不断扩大的感知范围。在四个系统变体中，我们根据人工标注评估了回合级工具决策的正确性，并收集了交互质量的主观评分。结果表明，用于主动感知的实时多模态大型语言模型和工具使用是实用具身情境对话的一个有希望的方向。

title: KGLAMP: Knowledge Graph-guided Language model for Adaptive Multi-robot Planning and Replanning
summary: Heterogeneous multi-robot systems are increasingly deployed in long-horizon missions that require coordination among robots with diverse capabilities. However, existing planning approaches struggle to construct accurate symbolic representations and maintain plan consistency in dynamic environments. Classical PDDL planners require manually crafted symbolic models, while LLM-based planners often ignore agent heterogeneity and environmental uncertainty. We introduce KGLAMP, a knowledge-graph-guided LLM planning framework for heterogeneous multi-robot teams. The framework maintains a structured knowledge graph encoding object relations, spatial reachability, and robot capabilities, which guides the LLM in generating accurate PDDL problem specifications. The knowledge graph serves as a persistent, dynamically updated memory that incorporates new observations and triggers replanning upon detecting inconsistencies, enabling symbolic plans to adapt to evolving world states. Experiments on the MAT-THOR benchmark show that KGLAMP improves performance by at least 25.5% over both LLM-only and PDDL-based variants.
tldr: KGLAMP uses a knowledge graph to guide a large language model in generating PDDL problem specifications for multi-robot planning, leading to improved performance in dynamic environments compared to LLM-only and PDDL-based approaches.
tldr_zh: KGLAMP使用知识图谱引导大型语言模型生成用于多机器人规划的PDDL问题规范，与仅使用LLM和基于PDDL的方法相比，在动态环境中提高了性能。
summary_zh: 异构多机器人系统越来越多地部署于需要具备多样能力的机器人们进行协作的长时程任务中。然而，现有的规划方法难以构建准确的符号化表示，并且在动态环境中维持计划的一致性。传统的PDDL规划器需要手动设计的符号模型，而基于LLM的规划器通常忽略智能体异构性和环境不确定性。我们提出KGLAMP，一个知识图谱引导的LLM规划框架，用于异构多机器人团队。该框架维护一个结构化的知识图谱，用于编码对象关系、空间可达性和机器人能力，从而引导LLM生成准确的PDDL问题描述。知识图谱作为一个持久的、动态更新的记忆，整合新的观测数据，并在检测到不一致时触发重新规划，使符号计划能够适应不断变化的世界状态。在MAT-THOR基准测试上的实验表明，KGLAMP的性能比纯LLM和基于PDDL的变体至少提高了25.5%。

title: Modular Safety Guardrails Are Necessary for Foundation-Model-Enabled Robots in the Real World
summary: The integration of foundation models (FMs) into robotics has accelerated real-world deployment, while introducing new safety challenges arising from open-ended semantic reasoning and embodied physical action. These challenges require safety notions beyond physical constraint satisfaction. In this paper, we characterize FM-enabled robot safety along three dimensions: action safety (physical feasibility and constraint compliance), decision safety (semantic and contextual appropriateness), and human-centered safety (conformance to human intent, norms, and expectations). We argue that existing approaches, including static verification, monolithic controllers, and end-to-end learned policies, are insufficient in settings where tasks, environments, and human expectations are open-ended, long-tailed, and subject to adaptation over time. To address this gap, we propose modular safety guardrails, consisting of monitoring (evaluation) and intervention layers, as an architectural foundation for comprehensive safety across the autonomy stack. Beyond modularity, we highlight possible cross-layer co-design opportunities through representation alignment and conservatism allocation to enable faster, less conservative, and more effective safety enforcement. We call on the community to explore richer guardrail modules and principled co-design strategies to advance safe real-world physical AI deployment.
tldr: This paper advocates for modular safety guardrails for foundation model-enabled robots to address the limitations of existing safety approaches in open-ended real-world scenarios, proposing a modular architecture with monitoring and intervention layers for action, decision, and human-centered safety.
tldr_zh: 本文倡导为基于基础模型的机器人设计模块化安全护栏，以解决现有安全方法在开放式现实场景中的局限性。论文提出了一种模块化架构，包含监控和干预层，以实现动作安全、决策安全和以人为中心的安全。
summary_zh: 基础模型（FMs）融入机器人技术加速了现实世界的部署，同时也带来了新的安全挑战，这些挑战源于开放式的语义推理和具身物理动作。这些挑战需要的安全概念超出了物理约束满足的范畴。在本文中，我们沿着三个维度来刻画具备基础模型的机器人的安全性：动作安全（物理可行性和约束合规性）、决策安全（语义和上下文适当性）以及以人为中心的安全（符合人类意图、规范和期望）。我们认为，现有的方法，包括静态验证、单片控制器和端到端学习策略，在任务、环境和人类期望是开放式、长尾且随着时间推移而变化的场景中是不够的。为了解决这一差距，我们提出模块化的安全防护栏，由监控（评估）层和干预层构成，作为跨自主堆栈实现全面安全性的架构基础。除了模块化之外，我们还强调了通过表征对齐和保守性分配实现跨层协同设计的可能性，以实现更快、更不保守和更有效的安全执行。我们呼吁社区探索更丰富的防护栏模块和有原则的协同设计策略，以推进安全现实世界的物理人工智能部署。

title: DADP: Domain Adaptive Diffusion Policy
summary: Learning domain adaptive policies that can generalize to unseen transition dynamics, remains a fundamental challenge in learning-based control. Substantial progress has been made through domain representation learning to capture domain-specific information, thus enabling domain-aware decision making. We analyze the process of learning domain representations through dynamical prediction and find that selecting contexts adjacent to the current step causes the learned representations to entangle static domain information with varying dynamical properties. Such mixture can confuse the conditioned policy, thereby constraining zero-shot adaptation. To tackle the challenge, we propose DADP (Domain Adaptive Diffusion Policy), which achieves robust adaptation through unsupervised disentanglement and domain-aware diffusion injection. First, we introduce Lagged Context Dynamical Prediction, a strategy that conditions future state estimation on a historical offset context; by increasing this temporal gap, we unsupervisedly disentangle static domain representations by filtering out transient properties. Second, we integrate the learned domain representations directly into the generative process by biasing the prior distribution and reformulating the diffusion target. Extensive experiments on challenging benchmarks across locomotion and manipulation demonstrate the superior performance, and the generalizability of DADP over prior methods. More visualization results are available on the https://outsider86.github.io/DomainAdaptiveDiffusionPolicy/.
tldr: The paper introduces Domain Adaptive Diffusion Policy (DADP) to improve zero-shot domain adaptation in reinforcement learning by disentangling static domain information and injecting domain representations into the diffusion process, achieving superior performance on locomotion and manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了领域自适应扩散策略（DADP），通过解耦静态领域信息并将领域表示注入扩散过程，来改进强化学习中的零样本领域自适应，并在运动和操作任务上取得了优越的性能。
summary_zh: 在基于学习的控制中，学习能够泛化到未见过的转移动态的领域自适应策略仍然是一个根本性的挑战。通过领域表示学习来捕捉领域特定信息，从而实现领域感知的决策，已经取得了显著进展。我们分析了通过动态预测学习领域表示的过程，并发现选择与当前步骤相邻的上下文会导致学习到的表示将静态领域信息与变化的动态属性纠缠在一起。这种混合会混淆条件策略，从而限制零样本自适应。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了DADP（领域自适应扩散策略），它通过无监督解耦和领域感知扩散注入实现稳健的自适应。首先，我们引入了滞后上下文动态预测，这是一种将未来状态估计置于历史偏移上下文的策略；通过增加这个时间间隙，我们通过过滤掉瞬态属性来无监督地解耦静态领域表示。其次，我们通过偏置先验分布并重新构建扩散目标，将学习到的领域表示直接集成到生成过程中。在运动和操作等具有挑战性的基准测试中进行的大量实验表明了DADP优于先前方法的性能和泛化能力。更多可视化结果可在 https://outsider86.github.io/DomainAdaptiveDiffusionPolicy/ 上获得。

title: VLS: Steering Pretrained Robot Policies via Vision-Language Models
summary: Why do pretrained diffusion or flow-matching policies fail when the same task is performed near an obstacle, on a shifted support surface, or amid mild clutter? Such failures rarely reflect missing motor skills; instead, they expose a limitation of imitation learning under train-test shifts, where action generation is tightly coupled to training-specific spatial configurations and task specifications. Retraining or fine-tuning to address these failures is costly and conceptually misaligned, as the required behaviors already exist but cannot be selectively adapted at test time. We propose Vision-Language Steering (VLS), a training-free framework for inference-time adaptation of frozen generative robot policies. VLS treats adaptation as an inference-time control problem, steering the sampling process of a pretrained diffusion or flow-matching policy in response to out-of-distribution observation-language inputs without modifying policy parameters. By leveraging vision-language models to synthesize trajectory-differentiable reward functions, VLS guides denoising toward action trajectories that satisfy test-time spatial and task requirements. Across simulation and real-world evaluations, VLS consistently outperforms prior steering methods, achieving a 31% improvement on CALVIN and a 13% gain on LIBERO-PRO. Real-world deployment on a Franka robot further demonstrates robust inference-time adaptation under test-time spatial and semantic shifts. Project page: https://vision-language-steering.github.io/webpage/
tldr: The paper introduces Vision-Language Steering (VLS), a training-free method to adapt pretrained robot policies to new environments by using vision-language models to guide action trajectories at inference time, demonstrating improved performance in simulated and real-world tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了视觉-语言引导（VLS），一种无需训练的方法，通过使用视觉-语言模型在推理时引导动作轨迹，使预训练的机器人策略适应新环境，并在模拟和真实世界任务中展示了改进的性能。
summary_zh: 预训练的扩散模型或流匹配策略在近障碍物、移动的支持表面或轻度杂乱环境下执行相同任务时，为何会失效？此类失败很少反映出缺乏运动技能，相反，它们暴露了在训练-测试分布偏移下模仿学习的局限性，即动作生成与训练特定的空间配置和任务规范紧密耦合。为此进行重新训练或微调既昂贵又在概念上不一致，因为所需的行为已经存在，但无法在测试时进行选择性调整。我们提出了视觉-语言引导（VLS），这是一个无需训练的框架，用于在推理时自适应冻结的生成式机器人策略。VLS将自适应视为一个推理时的控制问题，响应于超出分布的观察-语言输入，引导预训练的扩散模型或流匹配策略的采样过程，而无需修改策略参数。通过利用视觉-语言模型来合成轨迹可微的奖励函数，VLS指导去噪过程，使其朝向满足测试时空间和任务要求的动作轨迹。在模拟和真实世界的评估中，VLS始终优于先前的引导方法，在CALVIN数据集上实现了31%的改进，在LIBERO-PRO数据集上实现了13%的提升。在Franka机器人上的真实世界部署进一步证明了在测试时空间和语义偏移下的鲁棒推理时自适应能力。项目主页：https://vision-language-steering.github.io/webpage/

title: Conformal Reachability for Safe Control in Unknown Environments
summary: Designing provably safe control is a core problem in trustworthy autonomy. However, most prior work in this regard assumes either that the system dynamics are known or deterministic, or that the state and action space are finite, significantly limiting application scope. We address this limitation by developing a probabilistic verification framework for unknown dynamical systems which combines conformal prediction with reachability analysis. In particular, we use conformal prediction to obtain valid uncertainty intervals for the unknown dynamics at each time step, with reachability then verifying whether safety is maintained within the conformal uncertainty bounds. Next, we develop an algorithmic approach for training control policies that optimize nominal reward while also maximizing the planning horizon with sound probabilistic safety guarantees. We evaluate the proposed approach in seven safe control settings spanning four domains -- cartpole, lane following, drone control, and safe navigation -- for both affine and nonlinear safety specifications. Our experiments show that the policies we learn achieve the strongest provable safety guarantees while still maintaining high average reward.
tldr: This paper introduces a probabilistic verification framework combining conformal prediction and reachability analysis for safe control of unknown dynamical systems, demonstrating strong safety guarantees and high reward in various control tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种概率验证框架，结合了共形预测和可达性分析，用于未知动力系统的安全控制。实验结果表明，该方法在多种控制任务中实现了强大的安全保证和较高的奖励。
summary_zh: 设计可证明安全的控制是可信自主化的核心问题。然而，此前大多数相关工作要么假设系统动力学是已知的或确定性的，要么假设状态和动作空间是有限的，这大大限制了应用范围。我们通过为未知动力学系统开发一种概率验证框架来解决这一局限性，该框架将共形预测与可达性分析相结合。具体来说，我们使用共形预测来获得每个时间步骤未知动力学的有效不确定性区间，然后通过可达性分析来验证在共形不确定性界限内是否维持了安全性。接下来，我们开发了一种算法方法，用于训练在优化标称奖励的同时，最大限度地提高具有可靠概率安全保证的规划horizon的控制策略。我们在涵盖四个领域的七个安全控制场景（包括倒立摆、车道保持、无人机控制和安全导航）中评估了所提出的方法，这些场景的安全规范既有仿射的也有非线性的。我们的实验表明，我们学习到的策略在保持高平均奖励的同时，实现了最强的可证明安全保证。

title: CodeScaler: Scaling Code LLM Training and Test-Time Inference via Execution-Free Reward Models
summary: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has driven recent progress in code large language models by leveraging execution-based feedback from unit tests, but its scalability is fundamentally constrained by the availability and reliability of high-quality test cases. We propose CodeScaler, an execution-free reward model designed to scale both reinforcement learning training and test-time inference for code generation. CodeScaler is trained on carefully curated preference data derived from verified code problems and incorporates syntax-aware code extraction and validity-preserving reward shaping to ensure stable and robust optimization. Across five coding benchmarks, CodeScaler improves Qwen3-8B-Base by an average of +11.72 points, outperforming binary execution-based RL by +1.82 points, and enables scalable reinforcement learning on synthetic datasets without any test cases. At inference time, CodeScaler serves as an effective test-time scaling method, achieving performance comparable to unit test approaches while providing a 10-fold reduction in latency. Moreover, CodeScaler surpasses existing reward models on RM-Bench not only in the code domain (+3.3 points), but also in general and reasoning domains (+2.7 points on average).
tldr: The paper introduces CodeScaler, an execution-free reward model for code LLMs that improves performance in both training and inference by leveraging carefully curated preference data and syntax-aware reward shaping, showing better performance and latency compared to execution-based methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为CodeScaler的、无需执行的Code LLM奖励模型。该模型通过利用精心策划的偏好数据和语法感知奖励塑造改进了训练和推理性能，并展现出比基于执行的方法更好的性能和更低的延迟。
summary_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）通过利用单元测试的基于执行的反馈推动了代码大型语言模型领域的最新进展，但其可扩展性从根本上受到高质量测试用例的可用性和可靠性的限制。我们提出了CodeScaler，一种无执行的奖励模型，旨在扩展代码生成的强化学习训练和测试时推理。CodeScaler基于精心策划的、源自已验证代码问题的偏好数据进行训练，并融合了语法感知的代码提取和保有效性的奖励塑造，以确保稳定且鲁棒的优化。在五个代码基准测试中，CodeScaler使Qwen3-8B-Base的性能平均提升了+11.72分，超过了基于二进制执行的强化学习+1.82分，并且能够在没有任何测试用例的情况下，在合成数据集上实现可扩展的强化学习。在推理时，CodeScaler作为一种有效的测试时扩展方法，实现了与单元测试方法相当的性能，同时将延迟降低了10倍。此外，CodeScaler在RM-Bench上的表现优于现有的奖励模型，不仅在代码领域（+3.3分），而且在通用和推理领域（平均+2.7分）也表现出色。

title: DRMOT: A Dataset and Framework for RGBD Referring Multi-Object Tracking
summary: Referring Multi-Object Tracking (RMOT) aims to track specific targets based on language descriptions and is vital for interactive AI systems such as robotics and autonomous driving. However, existing RMOT models rely solely on 2D RGB data, making it challenging to accurately detect and associate targets characterized by complex spatial semantics (e.g., ``the person closest to the camera'') and to maintain reliable identities under severe occlusion, due to the absence of explicit 3D spatial information. In this work, we propose a novel task, RGBD Referring Multi-Object Tracking (DRMOT), which explicitly requires models to fuse RGB, Depth (D), and Language (L) modalities to achieve 3D-aware tracking. To advance research on the DRMOT task, we construct a tailored RGBD referring multi-object tracking dataset, named DRSet, designed to evaluate models' spatial-semantic grounding and tracking capabilities. Specifically, DRSet contains RGB images and depth maps from 187 scenes, along with 240 language descriptions, among which 56 descriptions incorporate depth-related information. Furthermore, we propose DRTrack, a MLLM-guided depth-referring tracking framework. DRTrack performs depth-aware target grounding from joint RGB-D-L inputs and enforces robust trajectory association by incorporating depth cues. Extensive experiments on the DRSet dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.
tldr: The paper introduces a new RGBD Referring Multi-Object Tracking (DRMOT) task and a corresponding dataset (DRSet) to address the limitations of existing 2D RGB-based RMOT methods, along with a baseline model (DRTrack) demonstrating the benefits of RGBD fusion.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的RGBD指代多目标跟踪（DRMOT）任务和一个相应的数据集（DRSet），以解决现有基于2D RGB的RMOT方法的局限性。同时，论文还提出了一个基线模型（DRTrack），展示了RGBD融合的优势。
summary_zh: 基于语言描述的多目标跟踪 (RMOT) 旨在跟踪特定目标，对于机器人和自动驾驶等交互式人工智能系统至关重要。然而，现有的RMOT模型仅依赖于2D RGB数据，因此在复杂空间语义（例如，“离相机最近的人”）描述下准确检测和关联目标，以及在严重遮挡下维持可靠的身份变得具有挑战性，因为缺乏显式的3D空间信息。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的任务，RGBD引用多目标跟踪 (DRMOT)，它明确要求模型融合RGB、深度 (D) 和语言 (L) 模态，以实现3D感知跟踪。为了推进DRMOT任务的研究，我们构建了一个定制的RGBD引用多目标跟踪数据集，名为DRSet，旨在评估模型在空间语义接地和跟踪方面的能力。具体来说，DRSet包含来自187个场景的RGB图像和深度图，以及240个语言描述，其中56个描述包含了深度相关的信息。此外，我们提出了DRTrack，一种基于多模态大语言模型 (MLLM) 指导的深度引用跟踪框架。DRTrack从联合RGB-D-L输入中执行深度感知的目标接地，并通过结合深度线索来实施鲁棒的轨迹关联。在DRSet数据集上的大量实验证明了我们框架的有效性。

title: Rethinking the Design Space of Reinforcement Learning for Diffusion Models: On the Importance of Likelihood Estimation Beyond Loss Design
summary: Reinforcement learning has been widely applied to diffusion and flow models for visual tasks such as text-to-image generation. However, these tasks remain challenging because diffusion models have intractable likelihoods, which creates a barrier for directly applying popular policy-gradient type methods. Existing approaches primarily focus on crafting new objectives built on already heavily engineered LLM objectives, using ad hoc estimators for likelihood, without a thorough investigation into how such estimation affects overall algorithmic performance. In this work, we provide a systematic analysis of the RL design space by disentangling three factors: i) policy-gradient objectives, ii) likelihood estimators, and iii) rollout sampling schemes. We show that adopting an evidence lower bound (ELBO) based model likelihood estimator, computed only from the final generated sample, is the dominant factor enabling effective, efficient, and stable RL optimization, outweighing the impact of the specific policy-gradient loss functional. We validate our findings across multiple reward benchmarks using SD 3.5 Medium, and observe consistent trends across all tasks. Our method improves the GenEval score from 0.24 to 0.95 in 90 GPU hours, which is $4.6\times$ more efficient than FlowGRPO and $2\times$ more efficient than the SOTA method DiffusionNFT without reward hacking.
tldr: This paper analyzes the design space of applying Reinforcement Learning to diffusion models for visual tasks, finding that a specific likelihood estimator (ELBO) is more crucial than the policy gradient objective itself for efficient and stable optimization.
tldr_zh: 本文分析了将强化学习应用于扩散模型以执行视觉任务的设计空间，发现特定的似然估计器 (ELBO) 对于高效且稳定的优化而言，比策略梯度目标本身更为关键。
summary_zh: 强化学习已被广泛应用于扩散模型和流模型，以处理文本生成图像等视觉任务。然而，由于扩散模型具有难以处理的似然性，这为直接应用流行的策略梯度类方法设置了障碍，因此这些任务仍然具有挑战性。现有方法主要侧重于构建基于已高度工程化的LLM目标的新的优化目标，使用特设的似然估计器，而没有彻底调查这种估计对整体算法性能的影响。在这项工作中，我们通过解耦三个因素对强化学习的设计空间进行了系统分析：i) 策略梯度目标，ii) 似然估计器，以及 iii) rollout采样方案。我们表明，采用仅从最终生成的样本计算的基于证据下界（ELBO）的模型似然估计器是实现有效、高效和稳定的强化学习优化的主要因素，超过了特定策略梯度损失函数的影响。我们使用SD 3.5 Medium在多个奖励基准上验证了我们的发现，并观察到所有任务中一致的趋势。我们的方法在90个GPU小时内将GenEval评分从0.24提高到0.95，效率比FlowGRPO高4.6倍，比SOTA方法DiffusionNFT高2倍，且没有进行奖励黑客。

title: Interpreting Physics in Video World Models
summary:
A long-standing question in physical reasoning is whether video-based models need to rely on factorized representations of physical variables in order to make physically accurate predictions, or whether they can implicitly represent such variables in a task-specific, distributed manner. While modern video world models achieve strong performance on intuitive physics benchmarks, it remains unclear which of these representational regimes they implement internally. Here, we present the first interpretability study to directly examine physical representations inside large-scale video encoders. Using layerwise probing, subspace geometry, patch-level decoding, and targeted attention ablations, we characterize where physical information becomes accessible and how it is organized within encoder-based video transformers.
  Across architectures, we identify a sharp intermediate-depth transition -- which we call the Physics Emergence Zone -- at which physical variables become accessible. Physics-related representations peak shortly after this transition and degrade toward the output layers. Decomposing motion into explicit variables, we find that scalar quantities such as speed and acceleration are available from early layers onwards, whereas motion direction becomes accessible only at the Physics Emergence Zone. Notably, we find that direction is encoded through a high-dimensional population structure with circular geometry, requiring coordinated multi-feature intervention to control. These findings suggest that modern video models do not use factorized representations of physical variables like a classical physics engine. Instead, they use a distributed representation that is nonetheless sufficient for making physical predictions.
tldr: This paper investigates how physical variables are represented within video world models, finding that they use distributed, rather than factorized, representations and identifying a "Physics Emergence Zone" in the network architecture.
tldr_zh: 本文研究了视频世界模型中物理变量的表征方式，发现它们使用分布式而非分解式表征，并确定了网络架构中一个名为“物理涌现区”的区域。
summary_zh:
物理推理领域长期存在一个问题：基于视频的模型是否需要依赖物理变量的因子化表示才能做出物理上准确的预测，或者它们是否可以以一种任务特定、分布式的的方式隐式地表示这些变量。虽然现代视频世界模型在直观物理基准上取得了优异的性能，但它们内部究竟实现了哪一种表示方式仍然不清楚。在此，我们展示了首个可解释性研究，旨在直接检查大规模视频编码器内部的物理表示。通过逐层探测、子空间几何、块级解码和目标注意力消融，我们刻画了物理信息在何处变得可访问以及它如何在基于编码器的视频Transformer中组织。

在不同架构中，我们识别出一个尖锐的中间深度跃迁——我们称之为物理涌现区——物理变量在此处变得可访问。与物理相关的表示在此跃迁后不久达到峰值，并向输出层衰减。将运动分解为显式变量，我们发现速度和加速度等标量量从早期层开始就可用，而运动方向仅在物理涌现区变得可访问。值得注意的是，我们发现方向是通过具有圆形几何结构的高维人口结构编码的，需要协调的多特征干预才能控制。这些发现表明，现代视频模型不像传统的物理引擎那样使用物理变量的因子化表示。相反，它们使用一种分布式表示，但这种表示足以进行物理预测。

title: WideSeek-R1: Exploring Width Scaling for Broad Information Seeking via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have largely focused on depth scaling, where a single agent solves long-horizon problems with multi-turn reasoning and tool use. However, as tasks grow broader, the key bottleneck shifts from individual competence to organizational capability. In this work, we explore a complementary dimension of width scaling with multi-agent systems to address broad information seeking. Existing multi-agent systems often rely on hand-crafted workflows and turn-taking interactions that fail to parallelize work effectively. To bridge this gap, we propose WideSeek-R1, a lead-agent-subagent framework trained via multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) to synergize scalable orchestration and parallel execution. By utilizing a shared LLM with isolated contexts and specialized tools, WideSeek-R1 jointly optimizes the lead agent and parallel subagents on a curated dataset of 20k broad information-seeking tasks. Extensive experiments show that WideSeek-R1-4B achieves an item F1 score of 40.0% on the WideSearch benchmark, which is comparable to the performance of single-agent DeepSeek-R1-671B. Furthermore, WideSeek-R1-4B exhibits consistent performance gains as the number of parallel subagents increases, highlighting the effectiveness of width scaling.
tldr: The paper introduces WideSeek-R1, a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework using width scaling (multiple subagents) to improve broad information seeking with LLMs, achieving comparable performance to a much larger single agent model.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了WideSeek-R1，一个多智能体强化学习框架，通过宽度扩展（多个子智能体）来提升LLM的广泛信息检索能力，其性能与更大的单智能体模型相当。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展主要集中于深度缩放，即单个智能体通过多轮推理和工具使用来解决长程问题。然而，随着任务范围的扩大，关键瓶颈从个体能力转变为组织能力。在这项工作中，我们探索了使用多智能体系统的宽度缩放，以解决广泛的信息搜寻问题。现有的多智能体系统通常依赖于手工设计的流程和轮流交互，无法有效地并行化工作。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了 WideSeek-R1，一种通过多智能体强化学习（MARL）训练的领导者智能体-子智能体框架，旨在协同可扩展的编排和并行执行。通过利用具有隔离上下文和专用工具的共享 LLM，WideSeek-R1 在一个包含 2 万个广泛信息搜寻任务的精心策划数据集上共同优化领导者智能体和并行子智能体。大量实验表明，WideSeek-R1-4B 在 WideSearch 基准测试中实现了 40.0% 的项目 F1 分数，可与单智能体 DeepSeek-R1-671B 的性能相媲美。此外，WideSeek-R1-4B 随着并行子智能体数量的增加表现出持续的性能提升，突出了宽度缩放的有效性。

title: From Assumptions to Actions: Turning LLM Reasoning into Uncertainty-Aware Planning for Embodied Agents
summary: Embodied agents operating in multi-agent, partially observable, and decentralized environments must plan and act despite pervasive uncertainty about hidden objects and collaborators' intentions. Recent advances in applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to embodied agents have addressed many long-standing challenges, such as high-level goal decomposition and online adaptation. Yet, uncertainty is still primarily mitigated through frequent inter-agent communication. This incurs substantial token and time costs, and can disrupt established workflows, when human partners are involved. We introduce PCE, a Planner-Composer-Evaluator framework that converts the fragmented assumptions latent in LLM reasoning traces into a structured decision tree. Internal nodes encode environment assumptions and leaves map to actions; each path is then scored by scenario likelihood, goal-directed gain, and execution cost to guide rational action selection without heavy communication. Across two challenging multi-agent benchmarks (C-WAH and TDW-MAT) and three diverse LLM backbones, PCE consistently outperforms communication-centric baselines in success rate and task efficiency while showing comparable token usage. Ablation results indicate that the performance gains obtained by scaling model capacity or reasoning depth persist even when PCE is applied, while PCE consistently raises the baseline across both capacity and reasoning-depth scales, confirming that structured uncertainty handling complements both forms of scaling. A user study further demonstrates that PCE produces communication patterns that human partners perceive as more efficient and trustworthy. Together, these results establish a principled route for turning latent LLM assumptions into reliable strategies for uncertainty-aware planning.
tldr: This paper introduces PCE, a framework that converts LLM reasoning into a decision tree to handle uncertainty in multi-agent embodied environments, outperforming communication-centric approaches in task success and efficiency while maintaining similar token usage.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为PCE的框架，该框架将LLM的推理转换为决策树，以处理多智能体具身环境中的不确定性。PCE在任务成功率和效率方面优于以通信为中心的方法，同时保持了相似的token使用量。
summary_zh: 在多智能体、部分可观测和去中心化的环境中运行的具身智能体，必须在普遍存在关于隐藏物体和协作者意图的不确定性的情况下进行规划和行动。近期将大型语言模型（LLMs）应用于具身智能体的进展已经解决了许多长期存在的挑战，例如高层目标分解和在线适应。然而，不确定性仍然主要通过频繁的智能体间通信来缓解。这会产生大量的token和时间成本，并且在涉及人类伙伴时可能会扰乱既定的工作流程。我们引入了PCE，一个规划器-组合器-评估器框架，该框架将LLM推理轨迹中潜在的碎片化假设转化为结构化的决策树。内部节点编码环境假设，叶节点映射到动作；然后通过场景可能性、目标导向增益和执行成本来对每条路径进行评分，以指导理性的动作选择，而无需大量的通信。在两个具有挑战性的多智能体基准测试（C-WAH和TDW-MAT）和三个不同的LLM主干网络上，PCE在成功率和任务效率方面始终优于以通信为中心的基线，同时表现出相当的token使用率。消融实验结果表明，即使应用PCE，通过扩展模型容量或推理深度获得的性能提升仍然存在，同时PCE在容量和推理深度尺度上始终提高基线，证实了结构化的不确定性处理是对这两种形式扩展的补充。一项用户研究进一步表明，PCE产生的通信模式被人类伙伴认为更有效和值得信赖。总之，这些结果为将潜在的LLM假设转化为用于不确定性感知规划的可靠策略建立了一条原则性的路线。

title: Agent-Omit: Training Efficient LLM Agents for Adaptive Thought and Observation Omission via Agentic Reinforcement Learning
summary: Managing agent thought and observation during multi-turn agent-environment interactions is an emerging strategy to improve agent efficiency. However, existing studies treat the entire interaction trajectories equally, overlooking the thought necessity and observation utility varies across turns. To this end, we first conduct quantitative investigations into how thought and observation affect agent effectiveness and efficiency. Based on our findings, we propose Agent-Omit, a unified training framework that empowers LLM agents to adaptively omit redundant thoughts and observations. Specifically, we first synthesize a small amount of cold-start data, including both single-turn and multi-turn omission scenarios, to fine-tune the agent for omission behaviors. Furthermore, we introduce an omit-aware agentic reinforcement learning approach, incorporating a dual sampling mechanism and a tailored omission reward to incentivize the agent's adaptive omission capability. Theoretically, we prove that the deviation of our omission policy is upper-bounded by KL-divergence. Experimental results on five agent benchmarks show that our constructed Agent-Omit-8B could obtain performance comparable to seven frontier LLM agent, and achieve the best effectiveness-efficiency trade-off than seven efficient LLM agents methods. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/usail-hkust/Agent-Omit.
tldr: The paper introduces Agent-Omit, a framework using agentic reinforcement learning to train LLM agents to adaptively omit redundant thoughts and observations, improving efficiency without sacrificing performance. Experimental results show it achieves state-of-the-art effectiveness-efficiency trade-offs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 Agent-Omit, 一个使用 Agentic 强化学习的框架，用于训练 LLM 智能体自适应地忽略冗余的思考和观察，从而提高效率且不牺牲性能。实验结果表明，它实现了最先进的有效性-效率权衡。
summary_zh: 在多轮Agent-环境交互过程中管理Agent的思考和观察是一种新兴的提升Agent效率的策略。然而，现有研究平等地对待整个交互轨迹，忽略了不同轮次之间思考必要性和观察效用的差异。为此，我们首先对思考和观察如何影响Agent的有效性和效率进行了定量研究。基于我们的发现，我们提出了Agent-Omit，一个统一的训练框架，使LLM Agent能够自适应地省略冗余的思考和观察。具体而言，我们首先合成少量冷启动数据，包括单轮和多轮省略场景，以对Agent进行微调，使其具备省略行为。此外，我们引入了一种省略感知的Agent强化学习方法，结合双重采样机制和定制的省略奖励，以激励Agent的自适应省略能力。理论上，我们证明了我们的省略策略的偏差以KL散度为上界。在五个Agent基准测试上的实验结果表明，我们构建的Agent-Omit-8B可以获得与七个前沿LLM Agent相当的性能，并且比七种高效LLM Agent方法实现了最佳的有效性-效率权衡。我们的代码和数据可在https://github.com/usail-hkust/Agent-Omit 获取。

title: Empirical-MCTS: Continuous Agent Evolution via Dual-Experience Monte Carlo Tree Search
summary: Inference-time scaling strategies, particularly Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS), have significantly enhanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, current approaches remain predominantly stateless, discarding successful reasoning patterns after each problem instance and failing to mimic the empirical accumulation of wisdom characteristic of human problem-solving. To bridge this gap, we introduce Empirical-MCTS, a dual-loop framework that transforms stateless search into a continuous, non-parametric learning process. The framework unifies local exploration with global memory optimization through two novel mechanisms: Pairwise-Experience-Evolutionary Meta-Prompting (PE-EMP) and a Memory Optimization Agent. PE-EMP functions as a reflexive optimizer within the local search, utilizing pairwise feedback to dynamically synthesize adaptive criteria and evolve meta-prompts (system prompts) in real-time. Simultaneously, the Memory Optimization Agent manages a global repository as a dynamic policy prior, employing atomic operations to distill high-quality insights across problems. Extensive evaluations on complex reasoning benchmarks, including AIME25, ARC-AGI-2, and MathArena Apex, demonstrate that Empirical-MCTS significantly outperforms both stateless MCTS strategies and standalone experience-driven agents. These results underscore the critical necessity of coupling structured search with empirical accumulation for mastering complex, open-ended reasoning tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces Empirical-MCTS, a novel MCTS framework that incorporates continuous, non-parametric learning by accumulating experiences via meta-prompt evolution and memory optimization, demonstrating significant improvements on complex reasoning benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Empirical-MCTS，一种新型的MCTS框架，它通过元提示进化和记忆优化积累经验，从而实现连续的、非参数的学习，并在复杂的推理基准测试中表现出显著的改进。
summary_zh: 推理时期的缩放策略，特别是蒙特卡洛树搜索(MCTS)，已经显著增强了大型语言模型(LLMs)的推理能力。然而，目前的方法主要保持无状态，在每个问题实例后丢弃成功的推理模式，无法模仿人类解决问题时经验积累的智慧。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了经验-MCTS(Empirical-MCTS)，一种双循环框架，将无状态搜索转换为连续的、非参数的学习过程。该框架通过两种新颖的机制统一了局部探索和全局记忆优化：成对经验进化元提示(PE-EMP)和一个记忆优化代理。PE-EMP作为局部搜索中的反射优化器，利用成对反馈来动态合成自适应标准，并实时进化元提示（系统提示）。与此同时，记忆优化代理将全局存储库管理为一个动态策略先验，采用原子操作来提炼跨问题的高质量洞察。在包括AIME25、ARC-AGI-2和MathArena Apex在内的复杂推理基准上的广泛评估表明，经验-MCTS显著优于无状态MCTS策略和独立的经验驱动代理。这些结果强调了将结构化搜索与经验积累相结合，对于掌握复杂、开放式推理任务的关键必要性。

title: From Helpfulness to Toxic Proactivity: Diagnosing Behavioral Misalignment in LLM Agents
summary: The enhanced capabilities of LLM-based agents come with an emergency for model planning and tool-use abilities. Attributing to helpful-harmless trade-off from LLM alignment, agents typically also inherit the flaw of "over-refusal", which is a passive failure mode. However, the proactive planning and action capabilities of agents introduce another crucial danger on the other side of the trade-off. This phenomenon we term "Toxic Proactivity'': an active failure mode in which an agent, driven by the optimization for Machiavellian helpfulness, disregards ethical constraints to maximize utility. Unlike over-refusal, Toxic Proactivity manifests as the agent taking excessive or manipulative measures to ensure its "usefulness'' is maintained. Existing research pays little attention to identifying this behavior, as it often lacks the subtle context required for such strategies to unfold. To reveal this risk, we introduce a novel evaluation framework based on dilemma-driven interactions between dual models, enabling the simulation and analysis of agent behavior over multi-step behavioral trajectories. Through extensive experiments with mainstream LLMs, we demonstrate that Toxic Proactivity is a widespread behavioral phenomenon and reveal two major tendencies. We further present a systematic benchmark for evaluating Toxic Proactive behavior across contextual settings.
tldr: This paper introduces the concept of 'Toxic Proactivity' in LLM agents, where they exhibit unethical behavior to maximize perceived usefulness, and presents a novel evaluation framework for identifying this behavior.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了LLM智能体中的“有毒的主动性”概念，即它们为了最大化感知的有用性而表现出不道德的行为，并提出了一个用于识别这种行为的新型评估框架。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型（LLM）的智能体的增强能力伴随着对模型规划和工具使用能力的需求。由于LLM对齐的有用性-无害性权衡，智能体通常也继承了“过度拒绝”的缺陷，这是一种被动的失效模式。然而，智能体积极的规划和行动能力在权衡的另一端引入了另一种关键危险。我们将这一现象称为“有毒主动性”：一种主动的失效模式，其中智能体受马基雅维利式有用性优化的驱动，无视伦理约束以最大化效用。与过度拒绝不同，有毒主动性表现为智能体采取过度或操控性措施来确保其“有用性”得以维持。现有研究很少关注识别这种行为，因为它通常缺乏展开此类策略所需的微妙上下文。为了揭示这种风险，我们引入了一种基于双重模型之间困境驱动交互的新型评估框架，从而能够模拟和分析智能体在多步骤行为轨迹上的行为。通过对主流LLM进行的大量实验，我们证明了有毒主动性是一种普遍存在的行为现象，并揭示了两种主要倾向。我们进一步提出了一个系统的基准，用于评估不同上下文中的有毒主动行为。

title: OMG-Agent: Toward Robust Missing Modality Generation with Decoupled Coarse-to-Fine Agentic Workflows
summary: Data incompleteness severely impedes the reliability of multimodal systems. Existing reconstruction methods face distinct bottlenecks: conventional parametric/generative models are prone to hallucinations due to over-reliance on internal memory, while retrieval-augmented frameworks struggle with retrieval rigidity. Critically, these end-to-end architectures are fundamentally constrained by Semantic-Detail Entanglement -- a structural conflict between logical reasoning and signal synthesis that compromises fidelity. In this paper, we present \textbf{\underline{O}}mni-\textbf{\underline{M}}odality \textbf{\underline{G}}eneration Agent (\textbf{OMG-Agent}), a novel framework that shifts the paradigm from static mapping to a dynamic coarse-to-fine Agentic Workflow. By mimicking a \textit{deliberate-then-act} cognitive process, OMG-Agent explicitly decouples the task into three synergistic stages: (1) an MLLM-driven Semantic Planner that resolves input ambiguity via Progressive Contextual Reasoning, creating a deterministic structured semantic plan; (2) a non-parametric Evidence Retriever that grounds abstract semantics in external knowledge; and (3) a Retrieval-Injected Executor that utilizes retrieved evidence as flexible feature prompts to overcome rigidity and synthesize high-fidelity details. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that OMG-Agent consistently surpasses state-of-the-art methods, maintaining robustness under extreme missingness, e.g., a $2.6$-point gain on CMU-MOSI at $70$\% missing rates.
tldr: The paper introduces OMG-Agent, a novel framework for robust missing modality generation that uses a decoupled coarse-to-fine agentic workflow to improve fidelity and overcome limitations of existing methods. It outperforms state-of-the-art methods, especially under high missingness rates.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为OMG-Agent的新框架，用于鲁棒地生成缺失模态数据。该框架采用解耦的粗到细的智能体工作流程来提高保真度，并克服现有方法的局限性。该方法优于最先进的方法，尤其是在高缺失率的情况下。
summary_zh: 数据不完整性严重影响多模态系统的可靠性。现有的重建方法面临着不同的瓶颈：传统的参数化/生成模型由于过度依赖内部记忆而容易产生幻觉，而检索增强框架则难以克服检索刚性。关键的是，这些端到端架构从根本上受到语义-细节纠缠的限制——一种逻辑推理和信号合成之间的结构性冲突，会损害保真度。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，即全模态生成代理 (OMG-Agent)，它将范式从静态映射转变为动态的由粗到精的代理工作流。通过模仿一种“深思熟虑后行动”的认知过程，OMG-Agent 将任务显式地解耦为三个协同阶段：（1）一个由 MLLM 驱动的语义规划器，通过渐进式上下文推理解决输入歧义，从而创建一个确定性的结构化语义计划；（2）一个非参数化证据检索器，将抽象语义扎根于外部知识；以及（3）一个检索注入的执行器，它利用检索到的证据作为灵活的特征提示，以克服刚性并合成高保真细节。在多个基准上的大量实验表明，OMG-Agent 始终超越最先进的方法，并在极端缺失情况下保持稳健性，例如，在缺失率达到 70% 时，在 CMU-MOSI 上获得了 2.6 个百分点的提升。

title: A$^2$-LLM: An End-to-end Conversational Audio Avatar Large Language Model
summary: Developing expressive and responsive conversational digital humans is a cornerstone of next-generation human-computer interaction.
